name: corrupted-temple
summary: A former temple given over to corruption — icons defaced, holy fixtures inverted or broken, a new unwholesome faith overlaid on the bones of the old. Layered desecration.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: long — bones of a nave remain, but the layout has been violated

primary_palette.note: the original altar overturned; a new necrotic one erected on top
a new idol of a malignant power replacing the defaced effigy
a black monolith planted through the old altar stone
the old altar kept but chained, used for bindings and bloodletting

story_prompts: a congregation of the new faith mid-service — robed figures chanting in the defiled nave
long abandoned even by the defilers — the second faith is also gone, only the ruin remains
reclamation underway — original priests attempting to purify, chalked sigils of warding half-complete
the desecration is recent — paint still wet on the walls, blood not yet dry on the altar
the corruption is spreading — a visible miasma, mushrooms and bone-growths on the pillars

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: low and sickly (0.15-0.25) — green, red, or blue-pale tint; the warm candles replaced with wrong-colored fires

anti_patterns: the layering must show — original features broken but visible beneath the new ones
never fully clean — this is desecration, there is ALWAYS rubble, blood, or defaced iconography
avoid uniform darkness; the wrong-colored fires should throw visible zones of unhealthy color
